title: lychnis triflora subsp. dawsonii
intro: Lychnis triflora subsp. dawsonii, also known as the Dawson's catchfly or Lychnis dawsonii, is a rare, endemic subspecies of flowering plant in the caryophyllaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lychnis triflora subsp. dawsonii include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and can also be applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
